issues.message: The apprenticeship petition form has inputs without programmatically-associated labels (missing_accessible_name / missing_form_label). The visible 'Given Name(s)', 'Family Name', etc. captions appear to be styled small-caps text rather than <label for=...> elements bound to the inputs.

Workshop directory cards trip the component-style consistency check (9/12 flagged). Visually the cards are intentionally varied (different specialty-tag counts, optional 'Accepting Apprentices' chip), which matches the concept of a heterogeneous member directory. Acceptable as designed.

Icon-style consistency flags fire on pages with engraved house marks and plate ornaments. This is by design — the concept explicitly calls for unique engraved house marks per workshop and per-plate ornaments. Not a real defect.

Layout consistency scores are low (24-65) on several pages. This reflects the intentional editorial folio asymmetry (drop caps, marginal footnotes, tipped-in plates, two-column justified columns with figures inset), not a real alignment defect. Screenshots show clean editorial grid.

repair_instructions: In apprenticeship.html, wire up the petition form for accessibility: wrap each visible field caption (Given Name(s), Family Name, Age at Petitioning, Prior Bench Experience, Preferred Instrument Family, Testimony from a Member, Signed at) in a <label> element with a `for` attribute matching the id of its input/select/textarea, or alternatively give each input an `aria-label` / `aria-labelledby` pointing at the existing caption. Keep the engraved small-caps visual styling on the label.
